meta.subject: Principles of Accounts
meta.philosophy: The syllabus for Principles of Accounts equips learners with knowledge, skills and positive values to be able to engage in and contribute to national development. The subject covers topics such as accounting concepts, business transactions, ledgers, subsidiary books and financial statements, equipping learners with knowledge and skills to understand, record financial transactions and prepare financial statements.
meta.competence_learning: The Competence Based Curriculum utilises a learner-centred approach, which entails that the teaching and learning process is learner-centred in nature. The emphasis is on what learners are expected to do rather than what they are expected to know, ensuring that learners develop competences that can contribute to the development of society.
goals: Record Business Transactions in the Subsidiary Books
Classify Capital and Revenue Receipts and Expenditure
Prepare the Statement of Profit or Loss
Draw up the Statement of Financial Position using the accounting equation method

subtopics: 2.1.1 Types of Books of Prime Entry
specific_outcomes: 2.1.1.1 Record Business Transactions in the Books of Prime Entry
2.1.1.2 Post Business Transactions to the Ledger

topics.topic_title: CAPITAL AND REVENUE RECEIPTS AND EXPENDITURE
subtopics: 2.3.1 Capital and Revenue Receipts and Expenditure
specific_outcomes: 2.3.1.1 Classify Capital and Revenue Receipts and Expenditure

topics.topic_title: FINANCIAL STATEMENTS OF A SOLE TRADER
subtopics: 2.4.1 Statement of Profit or Loss (Income Statement)
specific_outcomes: 2.4.1.1 Prepare the Statement of Profit or Loss
2.4.2.1 Prepare the Statement of Financial Position/Balance Sheet
